video_title: SpaceX IPO Mania and Ethena’s Strategic Pivot
gist: The 0xResearch team analyzes the record SpaceX IPO and its liquidity-sucking effect on Bitcoin (priced at ~$177 on Hyperliquid vs a $135 IPO price), argues on-chain indicators point to a Bitcoin bottom near realized price (~$53K) by Q4, and breaks down Ethena's pivot from a basis-trade wrapper to a diversified yield curator (institutional lending, CLOs, DeFi lending) with implications for the ENA token and a potential Coinbase distribution deal.

themes.theme: SpaceX IPO mechanics and Hyperliquid pre-IPO markets

insights.claim: SpaceX's IPO is the largest ever, priced at $135/share, and is trading around $177/share on Hyperliquid's pre-IPO market, signaling the market expects a premium to the IPO price.
insights.speaker: Danny
insights.so_what: A large premium on the pre-IPO market suggests a likely pop at open; traders can watch for convergence between the registered security price and the Hyperliquid price.
insights.quote: it's being priced at around 177 a share where the IPO price came in at 135

insights.claim: Hyperliquid's SpaceX pre-IPO market is seeing ~$373M in 24-hour volume and just under $300M in open interest, up ~4x from the prior day, already ranking as a top-10 HIP3 market before going live.
insights.speaker: Luke
insights.so_what: Demonstrates Hyperliquid's growing role as a price-discovery venue for pre-IPO equities; volume could reach up to $3B/day on this single market.
insights.quote: volumes at 373 mil over the past 24 hours and uh just short of 300 mil in open interest and that's up about 4x from this time yesterday

insights.claim: In prior pre-IPO markets, the Hyperliquid price prices a premium to the IPO price, and once the registered security goes live the trading price converges to the Hyperliquid price (as seen with Cerebras).
insights.speaker: Luke
insights.so_what: Provides a repeatable mental model for trading IPO opens using on-chain pre-IPO markets as a leading indicator.
insights.quote: once trading goes live on the registered security, that trading price converges to the hyperlquid price

insights.claim: SpaceX could become one of the most valuable companies in the world within a lifetime, but it could also trade 80% off its IPO price within two years.
insights.speaker: Luke
insights.so_what: Frames the IPO as a long-term winner but a short-term high-FDV trap to avoid bidding on day one.
insights.quote: I also wouldn't be surprised to see it 80% off its IPO price within two years or so

insights.claim: The SpaceX IPO resembles a low-float, high-FDV token launch where the narrative drives the price higher initially but many sellers are waiting to unlock and sell.
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Crypto-native pattern recognition suggests caution on early SpaceX price action despite the hype.
insights.quote: this reminds me of like any of those lowflat high FDV launches

insights.claim: Anthropic and OpenAI have reportedly already submitted confidential IPO filings, raising the prospect of a wave of large equity liquidity hitting markets in the coming months.
insights.speaker: Danny
insights.so_what: A cluster of mega-IPOs could repeatedly drain speculative capital away from crypto in coming months.
insights.quote: We've got um confidential filings from Anthropic and OpenAI supposedly already in

themes.theme: Bitcoin weakness and IPO liquidity drain

insights.claim: Bitcoin ETFs have seen over five consecutive weeks of net outflows totaling more than $5 billion, with the most recent day alone around $200 million in outflows.
insights.speaker: Carlos
insights.so_what: Sustained traditional-investor selling is a key bearish flow signal; watch for an ETF flow reversal as a turn indicator.
insights.quote: we have over I think it's five consecutive weeks at this point of consecutive uh outflows for Bitcoin. Uh it's over five billion I believe in outflows

insights.claim: The SpaceX IPO is analogized to the Trump coin launch for equity markets, sucking attention and liquidity out of risk assets including Bitcoin.
insights.speaker: Carlos
insights.so_what: Frames mega-IPOs as liquidity-absorbing events that disadvantage crypto in the near term.
insights.quote: the SpaceX IPO is kind of like the Trump coin launch for meme coins uh for equity markets

insights.claim: AI and semiconductor exposure has captured the 'hot ball of money' over the past year, with AI/semi indices ripping against Bitcoin, but that pair is becoming overbought and a relative-value trade back into crypto is expected.
insights.speaker: Luke
insights.so_what: Suggests a mean-reversion rotation trade from AI/semis back into crypto once the pair tops.
insights.quote: eventually at some point that pair gets overbought and like the relative value trade comes back to crypto in my opinion

insights.claim: Bitcoin miners transitioning from mining to AI compute could form a price bottom by reducing hash rate until mining economics become attractive again for new entrants.
insights.speaker: Danny
insights.so_what: Miner-to-AI offramping changes mining economics and could establish an equilibrium that supports a Bitcoin bottom.

insights.claim: MicroStrategy faces a trilemma among three competing interests—the Bitcoin price, the MSTR common stock price, and the Stretch preferred price—where one instrument must be sacrificed in favor of the other two.
insights.speaker: Luke
insights.so_what: Strategy appears willing to sacrifice common equity for senior/preferred debt holders, a bearish setup for MSTR common.
insights.quote: There's the Bitcoin price, the micro strategy common price and the stretch price. And it seems like one of those instruments kind of needs to get sacrificed in favor of the other two

insights.claim: Despite bearish narratives, MicroStrategy at large still isn't heavily levered and is not the marginal flow driving Bitcoin compared to ETF flows.
insights.speaker: Luke
insights.so_what: Tempers fears of a forced MSTR-driven Bitcoin liquidation cascade; ETF flows matter more.
insights.quote: strategy at large still isn't super levered and like relative to ETF flows. Um, he's not like the the marginal flow in size compared to what's driving ETFs

insights.claim: Saylor was reportedly the only net Bitcoin buyer last week, and his erratic moves (selling 32 BTC then buying BTC the next week) being tapped out could itself be a catalyst forming a market bottom.
insights.speaker: Danny
insights.so_what: Loss of Saylor as a persistent marginal buyer is a flow the market may need to digest before bottoming.

insights.claim: Bitcoin's realized price (the on-chain volume-weighted average cost basis of all coins) sits around $53K and is viewed as fair value; tagging a discount to that level would signal the bear-market low is in.
insights.speaker: Luke
insights.so_what: Gives a concrete accumulation level (~$53K, implying ~10-15% further downside from $63.3K) as a long-term value anchor.
insights.quote: if we tag a discount to that level, um I think bare market low is in that's kind of where where I'd be comfortable telling my mother that it's an okay time to allocate

insights.claim: Bitcoin is trading at range lows of $63.3K and may take out that low over the coming months, but from there downside risk is largely exhausted and risk shifts back to the upside, with the low likely set by Q4.
insights.speaker: Luke
insights.so_what: Frames Q4 as the timing for a cycle low and increasing optimism on the sector thereafter.
insights.quote: Bitcoin's teetering at range lows right now, 63.3K. Um, I wouldn't be surprised to see us take out that low. over the next few months. Um but from there I think like the downside risk is done

insights.claim: The MVRV Z-score is close to entering the historical-lows zone where spot price compresses toward realized price, an indicator that has reliably marked Bitcoin cycle bottoms.
insights.speaker: Luke
insights.so_what: Confluence of MVRV Z-score and realized price approaching lows strengthens the bottoming thesis.
insights.quote: we're pretty close to entering that zone that's marked historical lows

insights.claim: Bitcoin's one-week RSI dipping into oversold and the 200-week moving average are useful bottom signals, but in 2022 Bitcoin traded below the 200-week MA for about six months, so a V-shaped recovery should not be assumed.
insights.speaker: Carlos
insights.so_what: Accumulation zones can persist for months; investors should DCA patiently rather than expect an immediate bounce.
insights.quote: in 2022 when when it dipped below the 20 the 200 week moving average people were calling the bottom but it traded there for like six months

insights.claim: Levels of $57-58K (below realized-price targets) would be decent zones to start DCAing, especially after a large wick down or brutal alt price action over a week or two.
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Provides specific accumulation triggers; a 5-10% SPY drawdown could deliver these buy zones.
insights.quote: if it dips below 60 like 58 57 those are already pretty decent levels to start like DCAing into BDC

insights.claim: On-chain bottom indicators are more meaningful for Bitcoin than for equities because, as a subjectively-valued store of value, technicals drive buy/sell decisions more than forward earnings.
insights.speaker: Carlos
insights.so_what: Justifies leaning on technical/on-chain signals for Bitcoin allocation timing.
insights.quote: for Bitcoin it is a store of value assets. So because you just value it subjectively, I think the technicals actually matter a lot more

insights.claim: Historical green-zone buy signals coincide with existential events (3AC and FTX collapses in 2022), so the indicator firing low is not a mechanical buy because the asset's future is genuinely in question at those moments.
insights.speaker: Danny
insights.so_what: Cautions against treating backtested bottom zones as risk-free buys; regime shifts can invalidate past patterns.
insights.quote: all of these time periods coincide with those events where you're the market is actively making a decision about like, okay, are are we at a point where the future of this asset or this asset class is like going to go to zero

themes.theme: Ethena's pivot from basis trade to yield curator

insights.claim: Crypto basis has collapsed during the bear market, making the cash-and-carry trade unattractive, driving Ethena to reallocate reserves toward institutional lending, AAA CLOs, and equity/RWA/commodity perpetual futures basis.
insights.speaker: Luke
insights.so_what: Signals a structural business-model shift; Ethena's yield now depends on diversified non-basis sources.
insights.quote: during this bear that the basis on crypto has just collapsed and that cash and carry trade really isn't too attractive to a lot of market participants

insights.claim: Basis-trade returns have compressed across successive bull periods—roughly 20% (Trump election), then 15%, then 8-10% during the debt mania—as the trade gets commoditized by more institutions running it.
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Structural yield compression validates Ethena's diversification; basis yields will keep falling even in bull periods.
insights.quote: it was 20 then 15 and then last one during the debt mania it was around 8 to 10%

insights.claim: Ethena realistically has only three scalable capital-deployment options—institutional lending, AAA CLOs, and incentive-boosted DeFi lending—while gold and equity basis trades are out of the question due to insufficient open interest (gold has less open interest than Solana).
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Narrows the analyzable yield sources for SUSD and caps near-term diversification optionality.
insights.quote: gold has even lesser open interest than Solana. And like soul historically has taken no more than.5% of ETN's basis trade

insights.claim: Maple Finance's syrupUSDC grew from a few hundred million in AUM in early last year to a few billion dollars, with yields compressing from over 10% to closing in on 5% as institutional lenders like Falcon X and Coinbase Asset Management enter.
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Tracks institutional lending yield compression as a leading indicator for Ethena's achievable SUSD yield.
insights.quote: they started with I think just a few hundred million in AUM in Jan last year and now it's uh a few billion dollars already and the yields have started to come down

insights.claim: Maple's institutional lending is attractive because collateral can be escrowed at a third-party custodian (reducing exploit risk) and loans are fixed-rate/fixed-term (reducing interest-rate variance exposure seen in the RS exploit).
insights.speaker: Luke
insights.so_what: Explains why Maple sustained deposits despite the lending-space contraction; structural appeal for institutional capital.
insights.quote: collateral can be escrowed at third party custodian. um you you eliminate a lot of that that risk you might see elsewhere with with exploits

insights.claim: Ethena's diversification into CLOs and institutional lending is driven more by risk reduction than yield, since roughly half its stablecoins are in DeFi lending (with yields propped up by fading incentives) and a significant portion of the rest in T-bills.
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Reframes the pivot as de-risking concentration after the Kelp incident rather than chasing higher returns.
insights.quote: around half the stables are actually in D5 lending and uh a significant portion of the remaining stables are in T bills. So that reliance is a big issue

insights.claim: Ethena is converging on becoming an on-chain asset manager / hedge fund wrapped in a stablecoin, much like Sky has done for years—the difference being Ethena is vertically integrated while Sky outsources allocation to agents like Obex, Spark, and Grow.
insights.speaker: Carlos
insights.so_what: Positions Ethena as a yield curator competing with Sky; Sky is viewed as years ahead of the market on this model.
insights.quote: Athena is kind of converging upon what Sky has pursued for the past uh few years

insights.claim: Ethena must hold variable-duration, unwindable strategies to meet potential billions in redemptions within a 48-hour window during a deleveraging event while still balancing yield.
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Redemption-risk management constrains which yield strategies Ethena can use; institutional lending fits both liquidity and yield needs.
insights.quote: there's a few billion dollars worth of withdrawals within like a 48 hour window. You need to have sufficient strategies that can be unwound to meet those redemptions

insights.claim: The new diversified reserve strategies will likely deliver only a modest uptick in SUSD yield (~4.5-5%) while sharply reducing yield volatility, ending the era of clipping 20-30% fixed on SUSD principal tokens, though basis-trade optionality could still yield 7-8% in bullish ~3-month windows.
insights.speaker: Luke/Kunal
insights.so_what: Lower volatility makes SUSD less attractive to crypto-natives but more suitable as a stable yield product for institutions.
insights.quote: I fond memories of the opportunities to clip 20 to 30% fixed on an SUSD principal token. Like are those are those days gone for good?

insights.claim: Ethena retains optionality to switch back into the basis trade during bullish periods, an advantage that pure yield curators like Spark lack.
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Cyclical basis upside is a differentiator that could let SUSD outperform competitors in risk-on regimes.
insights.quote: Athena has the optionality to switch into the basis trade strategy where someone like spark doesn't

themes.theme: Coinbase distribution and the Morpho premium

insights.claim: A potential Coinbase-Ethena partnership could mirror the Coinbase-Morpho model, using Coinbase as a distribution channel to offer its large deposit base a ~1-2% spread above T-bill yields, materially boosting Ethena's TVL.
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Distribution, not yield, could be the key catalyst reversing Ethena's TVL outflows; watch for deal confirmation.
insights.quote: this Coinbase partnership announcement could be the one that kind of changes the game a little and kind of changes the the deposit base of Athena

insights.claim: Roughly 63% of Morpho's stablecoin deposits come from Coinbase users, a distribution advantage that has earned Morpho a large valuation premium versus other lending protocols despite near-zero value accrual to its token.
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Shows distribution and growth-mode market share can command a premium even without token cash flows—a template Ethena may replicate.
insights.quote: out of Morphos I think uh 63% of the stable coin deposits actually come from Coinbase users

themes.theme: ENA token value accrual and the fee-switch debate

insights.claim: Ethena's current hurdle to turn on the fee switch—a 6-7% spread over the ~3% SUSD yield—is unreasonable given the changed business model, and should be lowered to roughly a 1-2% spread above T-bills with an AUM threshold around $3.5-4 billion.
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Lowering the hurdle raises the probability of fee capture, which investors should discount less—forward guidance alone could rerate ENA.
insights.quote: One of the requirements was they need like a 6 to 7% spread over SUSDs which is at 3%. Like come on. I mean that's no longer reasonable

insights.claim: Modeling a 4% take on ENA at various TVL scenarios yields buybacks of roughly 3-7% of supply annually (at a ~0.40% lean), which is dwarfed by ~1.8% of ENA supply unlocking each month through 2028.
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Buybacks won't offset unlocks near-term, so the fee switch is mainly a signaling tool rather than a supply-absorption mechanism.
insights.quote: till 2028 I think around 1.8% of the silk supply gets unlocked each month. So this buy will be nothing close to the amount of unlocks

insights.claim: Even if buybacks are insignificant, turning on the fee switch is worth doing to signal to the market that the ENA token is tied to the protocol and not just a disconnected asset.
insights.speaker: Kunal
insights.so_what: Signaling alignment could improve ENA sentiment and valuation independent of cash-flow size.
insights.quote: having just telling signaling to the market that there's some sort of association is is good enough

insights.claim: Tokenized equities and businesses coming on chain will increasingly punish protocols whose tokens lack equity-like alignment, as token holders are repeatedly zeroed in acquisitions where value accrues elsewhere.
insights.speaker: Danny
insights.so_what: Token-to-business alignment (Morpho-style foundation/token unification) is becoming a key valuation discriminator for protocol tokens.
insights.quote: we've seen with with countless acquisitions and other deals over the past one two years now that have just been zeros for token holders

insights.claim: Growth-mode with no fee capture can be the optimal strategy to win market share (as Morpho demonstrates), so Ethena should first show execution on the reserve shift and SUSD APY improvement before recommunicating fee-switch metrics.
insights.speaker: Luke
insights.so_what: Suggests sequencing: prove execution and growth before monetizing, supporting a patient view on ENA value accrual.
insights.quote: growth mode and no fee capture can actually be a really really good strategy to get market share

insights.claim: ENA at some point must trade as a value token where unlocks are discounted, but it has not yet reached an attractive price despite progress in TVL and revenue.
insights.speaker: Carlos
insights.so_what: Implies continued caution on ENA until the market is willing to look through unlocks.
insights.quote: I do think that at some point ENA has to be a value token, right?
